title: INTENSIVE IN-HOME BEHAVIORAL HEALTH TREATMENT - 211info
text: OREGON COMMUNITY PROGRAMS INTENSIVE IN-HOME BEHAVIORAL HEALTH TREATMENT Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers intensive mental health services designed to help children remain safe and stable within their homes and communities. Services offered include psychiatric treatment, mental health therapy, care coordination, skills training, and peer support. Intensive In-Home Behavioral Health Treatment provides 2-4 hours of intensive, in-home services per week and typically lasts 3-6 months; though services can continue longer based on individual needs. 1170 Pearl Street Eugene, OR 97401 Eligibility: Ages 0-20 Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-5pm Email (541) 743-4340 Get Directions Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers Main phone (541) 743-4340 Email Address: [email protected] INTAKE PROCEDURE: Visit website or email for more information FEES: Accepts OHP DOCUMENTS: None LANGUAGES: English, interpretation by arrangement AREA SERVED: Lane County SITE HOURS: Monday-Friday 9am-6pm TRAVEL/LOCATION INFORMATION: Located on Lane Transit District downtown loop bus route, corner of 12th and Pearl. Services: Individual Counseling Individual Counseling * Children and Youth With Disabilities/Health Conditions Peer Mental Health Support Services Peer Mental Health Support Services * Children and Youth With Disabilities/Health Conditions Psychiatric Case Management Psychiatric Case Management * Children and Youth With Disabilities/Health Conditions Psychiatric Medication Services Psychiatric Medication Services * Children and Youth With Disabilities/Health Conditions Other Services Offered At This Location: COUNSELING SETTINGS - CONNECTIONS PROGRAM INDIVIDUAL AND FAMILY SUPPORT SERVICES - EARLY CHILDHOOD PROGRAMS INDIVIDUAL AND FAMILY SUPPORT SERVICES - SUPPORT & THERAPY GROUPS MUTUAL SUPPORT - FOSTER PARENT SUPPORT GROUP If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service.
